answer:
A.

Skeptical senior engineers trust first-person postmortems ("we did X, here's data") and distrust second-person fear-bait. B's "hiding in YOUR config" + round scary dollar figure pattern-matches to content marketing/clickbait — HN comments will mock the headline before reading. A promises a concrete, verifiable claim about your own system; 80% invites "show me the numbers," which is engagement you want.

Caveat: A only works if the post actually delivers the 80% with real breakdowns. If it's thin, both fail.

Optional strengthening: add specificity — "How We Cut Our AWS Bill 80% by Fixing One Autoscaling Config."
